annotations.label: Adjoint representation of a Lie group

anchor.snippet: the adjoint representation (or adjoint action ) of a Lie group G is a way of representing the elements of the group as linear transformations of the group's Lie algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib has the Lie-algebra-level `LieAlgebra.ad` but does not define the Lie group adjoint representation `Ad : G → Aut(g)`.

annotations.label: Adjoint representation of GL(n)

anchor.snippet: For example, if G is

annotations.note: No Lie group adjoint representation is defined, so the GL(n) example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Inner automorphism map Ψ

anchor.snippet: be the mapping g ↦ Ψ g , with Aut( G ) the automorphism group of G and Ψ g : G → G given by the inner automorphism

annotations.note: Mathlib has `MulAut.conj` for the abstract group inner-automorphism map, but not the smooth Lie group inner automorphism Ψ_g : G → Aut(G).

annotations.label: Ad_g as derivative of Ψ_g

anchor.snippet: For each g in G , define Ad g to be the derivative of Ψ g at the origin

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define Ad_g as the derivative at the identity of conjugation.

annotations.label: Adjoint representation of G

anchor.snippet: is a group representation called the adjoint representation of G

annotations.note: No Lie group-level adjoint representation `Ad : G → GL(g)` is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ad formula for linear Lie groups

anchor.snippet: If G is a linear Lie group , then the Lie algebra

annotations.note: No formula `Ad_g(X) = gXg⁻¹` for matrix Lie groups exists in Mathlib because Ad on Lie groups is not defined.

annotations.label: Adjoint as isotropy representation

anchor.snippet: Succinctly, an adjoint representation is an isotropy representation associated to the conjugation action of G around the identity element of G

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks the notion of isotropy representation of a Lie group action.

annotations.label: Adjoint representation of the Lie algebra (via derivative of Ad)

anchor.snippet: at the identity element gives the adjoint representation of the Lie algebra

annotations.note: `LieAlgebra.ad` defines ad directly on a Lie algebra, but not as the derivative of `Ad` at the identity since `Ad` is absent.

annotations.label: Ad exp(x) = exp(ad x)

anchor.snippet: Ad and ad are related through the exponential map

annotations.note: No Lie group exponential / Ad / ad relation theorem found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Adjoint endomorphism ad_x

anchor.section: Adjoint representation of a Lie algebra
anchor.snippet: It is called the adjoint endomorphism or adjoint action

annotations.note: `LieAlgebra.ad R L : L →ₗ⁅R⁆ Module.End R L` with `ad_apply : ad R L x y = ⁅x, y⁆` formalizes ad_x.

annotations.label: ad is a Lie algebra homomorphism (adjoint representation)

anchor.section: Adjoint representation of a Lie algebra
anchor.snippet: This last identity says that ad is a Lie algebra homomorphism

annotations.note: `LieAlgebra.ad` is typed as a Lie algebra homomorphism `L →ₗ⁅R⁆ Module.End R L`, so the homomorphism property is the definition's typing.

annotations.label: Kernel of ad is the center

anchor.section: Adjoint representation of a Lie algebra
anchor.snippet: The kernel of ad is the center of

annotations.note: `ad_ker_eq_center : (ad R L).ker = LieAlgebra.center R L` proves the kernel of the adjoint action equals the center.

annotations.label: ad_z is a derivation (Leibniz law)

anchor.section: Adjoint representation of a Lie algebra
anchor.snippet: the linear mapping

annotations.note: `LieAlgebra.ad_lie` states `(ad R L x) ⁅y, z⁆ = ⁅ad R L x y, z⁆ + ⁅y, ad R L x z⁆`, the derivation property, and `LieDerivation.ad` packages ad as a Lie derivation.

annotations.label: ad is differential of Ad at identity

anchor.section: Adjoint representation of a Lie algebra
anchor.snippet: When

annotations.note: Mathlib does not relate ad to a derivative of `Ad` because Lie group Ad is not formalized.

annotations.label: Leibniz-like formula for ad

anchor.section: Adjoint representation of a Lie algebra
anchor.snippet: There is the following formula similar to the Leibniz formula

annotations.note: `LieAlgebra.ad_pow_lie` (via `LieModule.toEnd_pow_lie`) gives the binomial-style expansion `(ad x)^n ⁅y,z⁆ = Σ choose ... ⁅(ad x)^i y, (ad x)^j z⁆`.

annotations.label: Matrix elements of ad via structure constants

anchor.snippet: The explicit matrix elements of the adjoint representation are given by the structure constants of the algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Mathlib.Algebra.Lie.Basis` discusses structure constants conceptually but does not formalize the matrix-of-ad-equals-structure-constants identity.

annotations.label: Adjoint representation of su(2) is so(3)

anchor.snippet: Thus, for example, the adjoint representation of su(2) is the defining representation of so(3)

annotations.note: No isomorphism between the adjoint representation of su(2) and the defining representation of so(3) is recorded in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Adjoint representation of abelian group is trivial

anchor.snippet: If G is abelian of dimension n , the adjoint representation of G is the trivial

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `IsLieAbelian` and trivial Lie modules, and ad is zero on abelian Lie algebras by `ad_apply`, but the Lie group statement is not present.

annotations.label: Adjoint for matrix Lie group

anchor.snippet: If G is a matrix Lie group

annotations.note: Lie-group-level adjoint for matrix groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Adjoint representation of SL(2, R)

anchor.snippet: If G is SL(2, R ) (real 2×2 matrices with determinant 1)

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the Lie subalgebra `sl` in `Mathlib.Algebra.Lie.Classical` but not the SL(2,ℝ) Lie-group adjoint representation example.

annotations.label: Kernel of Ad and faithfulness

anchor.snippet: If G is connected , the kernel of the adjoint representation coincides with the kernel of Ψ which is just the center of G

annotations.note: Kernel of group-level Ad as center of G is not formalized; only the analogous Lie algebra statement `ad_ker_eq_center` exists.

annotations.label: Adjoint group via Lie's third theorem

anchor.snippet: by Lie's third theorem , there is a connected Lie group

annotations.note: Lie's third theorem and the construction of the adjoint group are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Image of adjoint representation equals adjoint group

anchor.snippet: Now, if

annotations.note: No formalization of the adjoint group as image of Ad in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-zero weights form a root system

anchor.section: Roots of a semisimple Lie group
anchor.snippet: If G is semisimple , the non-zero weights of the adjoint representation form a root system

annotations.note: `LieAlgebra.IsKilling.rootSystem` constructs the root system from nonzero weights of the adjoint action of a Cartan subalgebra on a finite-dimensional Lie algebra with nondegenerate Killing form.

annotations.label: Root system of SL(n, R)

anchor.section: Roots of a semisimple Lie group
anchor.snippet: To see how this works, consider the case G = SL( n , R )

annotations.note: Although `Mathlib.Algebra.Lie.Classical.sl` exists, the explicit type-A root system of sl(n) is not assembled in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Root system of SL(2, R)

anchor.snippet: When computing the root system for one of the simplest cases of Lie Groups, the group SL(2, R ) of two dimensional matrices with determinant 1

annotations.note: `Mathlib.Algebra.Lie.Sl2` develops sl₂-triples, but the explicit A₁ root system computation for sl(2,ℝ) is not given.

annotations.label: Co-adjoint representation

anchor.snippet: The co-adjoint representation is the contragredient representation of the adjoint representation

annotations.note: Searches for `coadjoint`/`co_adjoint`/`contragredient` in Mathlib return no results.

annotations.label: Kirillov: co-adjoint orbits are symplectic manifolds

anchor.snippet: Alexandre Kirillov observed that the orbit of any vector in a co-adjoint representation is a symplectic manifold

annotations.note: No Kirillov orbit / symplectic-structure-on-coadjoint-orbits formalization in Mathlib.
